title: Aleuritopteris niphobola
taxonomy: Aleuritopteris niphobola is classified within the family Pteridaceae, commonly known as the brake fern family. This species belongs to the genus Aleuritopteris, which is a small group of ferns native to the mountainous regions of Asia. The classification of Aleuritopteris niphobola is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its simple, pinnate leaves and the presence of sori on the underside of the fronds. Within the Pteridaceae family, Aleuritopteris niphobola is often grouped together with other species that share similar characteristics. The classification of this species is an important aspect of understanding its evolutionary history and relationships with other ferns.

intro: Aleuritopteris niphobola, also known as the Himalayan varanji or varanji, is a type of fern plant native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its delicate fronds and unique growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and its extracts have been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
